text: Add circuit-breaker logic to supervisor-state.ts to reset or halt after N consecutive ERRORs to avoid retry ceiling
description: Current backoff in supervisor-state.ts increases but can still lead to repeated 'retry ceiling reached' in friction. Add logic: after e.g. 5 consecutive ERRORs without success, trigger a special state or log a limitation and suggest aim. Reset on success. This improves the supervisor in wrapped-agents for the ASI ideation aim.
reflection:
Completed per description and follow-up items:
- Circuit breaker with CIRCUIT_OPEN after threshold consecutive ERRORs, blocks retries, prompts for auto-propose/create_aim.
- closeCircuit integrated in setEnabled for recovery.
- Unit test added simulating ceiling and close on enable.
- Threshold configurable via AutonomyPolicy.maxConsecutiveErrors (default 5).
- Watchdog state handling supports via getState (CIRCUIT_OPEN defined).

Evidence: code changes, tests 63/63, type-check clean, commits. No more 'retry ceiling' without self-improvement trigger.

Ties to auto-propose and ASI supervisor reliability.

status.comment: Full logic, integration of closeCircuit on recovery, unit test for ceiling, configurable via policy. Type-check/tests pass, committed. Addresses retry ceiling in supervisor.
